items.title: Literary Prizewinners Are Facing AI Allegations. It Feels Like the New Normal
items.summary: Three of five regional winners of the prestigious Commonwealth Short Story Prize are suspected of relying on chatbots. They're certainly not alone.

items.title: California’s Wildfire Season Is Already Overactive
items.summary: Major fires are threatening homes and ecologically sensitive areas following a hot, dry winter.

items.title: Everything Announced at Google I/O 2026: Gemini, Search, Smart Glasses
items.summary: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.

items.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
items.summary: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

items.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
items.summary: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

items.title: How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
items.summary: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

items.title: 'Ebola has tortured us': Fear grips eastern DR Congo as deadly virus spreads
items.summary: The health minister has acknowledged that medics are playing catch-up with the virus after being slow to detect it.

items.title: Ebola outbreak may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
items.summary: Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

items.title: Key people smuggler arrested after BBC uncovered identity
items.summary: The 28-year-old's network is thought to have transported thousands of illegal migrants into the UK.

items.title: Fed to hike? When traders see a rate increase coming
items.summary: Traders on prediction market platforms see increasing odds that an interest rate hike from the central bank comes by July 2027.

items.title: Standard Chartered to cut over 15% of corporate functions roles as it targets higher returns
items.summary: Standard Chartered will trim corporate functions roles and target a more than 20% increase in income per employee by 2028 as it seeks stronger returns.

items.title: Nvidia earnings call drama: Will Jensen Huang talk 'Trump' and China chips after Xi summit?
items.summary: Both Nvidia's earnings and its commentary on its financial report are expected to be big tests for U.S. equities.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: GOP panel looks to advance legislation making daylight saving permanent
items.summary: Republicans on the House Energy and Commerce Committee are planning to move ahead with legislation that would make “springing forward” permanent across the U.S. Matthew VanHyfte, a spokesperson for the committee’s Republicans, told The Hill that the panel plans to notice on Wednesday evening a full markup of the bill that will “list the legislation that includes…

items.title: Trump golf resort manager advising on reflecting pool project
items.summary: A manager of one of President Trump’s golf courses has served as an unofficial adviser on the renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, an Interior Department spokesperson confirmed on Tuesday.  David Schutzenhofer helped recruit Greenwater Services for a $1.7 million no-bid contract with the Trump administration to install a permanent purification system in the…

items.title: Vance says Europe ‘overreacting’ to Pentagon halting Poland troop rotation
items.summary: Vice President Vance on Tuesday defended last week’s abrupt pause in a ​U.S. troop deployment to Poland, saying the brigade of 4,000 service members had been “delayed” to Europe and not withdrawn. Speaking at a White House briefing, Vance ​called the pause “a very small and very minor thing,” blaming the European media for “overreacting,” while…

items.title: Scientists found a smarter Mediterranean diet that slashes diabetes risk by 31%
items.summary: A large European study revealed that a lower-calorie Mediterranean diet paired with exercise and coaching dramatically reduced the risk of type 2 diabetes. Participants who made these lifestyle changes were 31% less likely to develop the disease over six years. They also lost more weight and trimmed their waistlines compared to those following a standard Mediterranean diet alone.

items.title: Rare graves reveal a lost world of Bronze Age Europe hidden for 3,000 years
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered remarkable new details about Bronze Age life in Central Europe by studying rare burials untouched by cremation. The research reveals communities experimenting with new foods, burial rituals, and cultural connections while largely staying rooted in their local homelands.

items.title: Hidden earthquake faults beneath Seattle may be more dangerous than expected
items.summary: A hidden network of earthquake faults running beneath Seattle may be far more active than scientists realized. New research reveals that smaller “secondary” faults in the Seattle Fault Zone appear to rupture roughly every 350 years — much more often than the massive main fault that has long worried geologists.

items.title: What freediving can reveal about human health — and our limits
items.summary: The practice of freediving is teaching physiologists how humans stretch their physical and mental limits, which in turn may improve treatments for lung and heart ailments.

items.title: AI-powered whale-spotting tech may help save San Francisco Bay’s gray whales
items.summary: An AI trained to use thermal images to detect whale body heat could help warn ships at risk of colliding with the marine mammals.

items.title: Damaged DNA can spread between human cells. What could that mean for cancer?
items.summary: DNA can voyage along intercellular highways called tunneling nanotubes. It’s a phenomenon that could potentially spread tumor DNA to healthy cells.
